entry.title: Updating Bank Accounts on Business Partner using SAP Service Layer on SAP Business One
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I’m trying to update the bank accounts of a Business Partner using SAP Service Layer. My JSON looks like this:

{
"CardCode": "P000454",
"BPBankAccounts": [
{
"AccountNo": "100005411",
"BankCode": "102301063",
"AccountName": "Robinson Grinding and Profiling, LLC JAS",
"Country": "US",
"InternalKey": 6999
}
]
}

When I perform a GET on the same Business Partner, the bank accounts segment returns this:

"BPBankAccounts": [
{
"LogInstance": 0,
"UserNo4": null,
"BPCode": "P000454",
"County": null,
"State": null,
"UserNo2": null,
"IBAN": "",
"ZipCode": null,
"City": null,
"Block": null,
"Branch": null,
"Country": "US",
"Street": null,
"ControlKey": "",
"UserNo3": null,
"BankCode": "102301063",
"AccountNo": "100005411",
"UserNo1": null,
"InternalKey": 6999,
"BuildingFloorRoom": null,
"BIK": null,
"AccountName": "Robinson Grinding and Profiling, LLC",
"CorrespondentAccount": null,
"Phone": null,
"Fax": null,
"CustomerIdNumber": "0",
"ISRBillerID": null,
"ISRType": 2,
"BICSwiftCode": "",
"ABARoutingNumber": null,
"MandateID": null,
"SignatureDate": null,
"MandateExpDate": null,
"SEPASeqType": null,
"U_SEI_LegalName": null,
"U_SEI_Moneda": null,
"U_SEI_TipoC": null,
"U_SEI_CCI": null,
"U_MSS_CtaDet": null
}
]

I included the "InternalKey" in my JSON to see if it helps identify the account, but I keep getting an error saying that the account already exists. How can I solve this issue?. If I use PATCH, it will include a new bank account. But if a use PUT, service layer will ask for the rest of the properties of the business partners which I don't intend to change. 

Many Thanks
SAP Business One, version for SAP HANA SAP Business One

entry.title: Re: Fetch Production Order after reading QM Notification...
entry.content:
Dear @inamullah,

The supported method in Public Cloud is to implement custom logic in Custom Fields and Logic for business context Notification_Header (Quality Notification). SAP documents this enhancement point specifically for Quality Notification apps and states that it is used to retrieve and change notification data when the notification is saved. Therefore for this requirement the supported design is to read the External Reference Number field in that save-time logic, derive the production order from the serial number and write the result back during the same save processing. SAP also documents I_SerialNumberManufacturingOrd as the CDS view that retrieves information on serial numbers used within manufacturing orders

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d1e58be39d884a0dbf75a7526a9acbf4/b392cc6291ea442a8c7636f05284dd24.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/8f9861166e914f61861801d9a8e8f448.html

The released SAP API documentation for Quality Notification should not be positioned as the primary method for this scenario. The defensible Public Cloud approach is save-time custom logic on the notification header. If the required target field is not available there the practical workaround is to store the derived production order in a custom field enabled for the quality notification apps.

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0629fd486efa4d339f8b10603b6f1e3f/9d9f91a2b3954ec48d1a12d54a0909a7.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0629fd486efa4d339f8b10603b6f1e3f/454808498fc344a8a08b912503d90ac0.html

Summary:

The supported pattern is Custom Fields and Logic on Notification_Header (Quality Notification), not a separate QM-notification update API. Read the serial number there, derive the order via I_SerialNumberManufacturingOrd and update the notification during the same save

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: 2ET: Correct CBC Org Structure for Sales Org / Plant / Shipping Point?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am a junior consultant working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and I am currently documenting and testing several scope items for a sales scope we want to offer. My current focus is 2ET – Sales Order Processing for Non-Stock Material.

1. What exactly do we do in the system?

I tested the 2ET process in our customizing tenant and compared the findings with our development tenant.

Systems:

  • Stark Tower = customizing tenant

  • Arc Reactor = development tenant

Relevant process / step:

  • Scope item 2ET – Sales Order Processing for Non-Stock Material

  • especially the organizational prerequisites needed so sales processing works correctly

Relevant apps / objects:

  • Manage Sales Orders – Version 2

  • CBC Organizational Structure

  • CBC: Assign Sales Organization - Distribution Channel - Plant

  • shipping-point related CBC activities such as:

    • Define Shipping Point

    • Assign Shipping Points

    • Assign Shipping Points According to Storage Location

    • Define Rule for Determination of Shipping Point

My original operational issue was that in sales-order processing I could not get a usable shipping-point result for DE01, so I started checking and adjusting the setup in the customizing tenant. That led me deeper into CBC and the organizational structure.

2. What do I expect?

I expect that if the organizational structure is built correctly, then a sales organization such as DE01 should appear in CBC activity "Assign Sales Organization - Distribution Channel - Plant" and should also function correctly in the application afterwards.

My goal is to understand the minimum required org structure / hierarchy so I know when the sales organization should appear in that CBC configuration and when it becomes functionally usable in the system. This is the main reason for my question.

3. What actually happens?

What I see is inconsistent:

  • In Organizational Structure, DE01 is visible:

    • Company Code DE01

    • Sales Organization DE01

    • Sales Area DE01 / DE / 01

    • Plant DE01

    • Shipping Point DE01

    • Storage Location DE01

  • However, in CBC activity "Assign Sales Organization - Distribution Channel - Plant", DE01 is not listed at all.

A colleague explained to me that this assignment only appears if the hierarchy is correct and complete. If that is true, then DE01 may be visually present in Organizational Structure, but still structurally incomplete from CBC’s point of view.

That is exactly the inconsistency I am trying to understand.

4. Suspected cause

My current assumption is that the issue is caused by an inconsistent org structure / hierarchy in the customizing tenant.

More specifically:

  • parts of the structure were visible, but not all relationships seemed equally solid

  • DE01 did not appear in the CBC assignment where I would expect it

  • earlier we also saw cases where plant / shipping-related relationships looked disconnected or incomplete in Stark compared with Arc

At one point we also worked on shipping-point-related setup and org connections, because the issue first looked like a shipping-point problem. Later, a colleague suggested comparing the setup with the development tenant. In that comparison, the Arc Reactor structure looked much cleaner and more coherent than Stark Tower.

Development vs. customizing comparison

In Arc Reactor, the structure looks like a proper reference:

  • Sales Organization GE01 is connected cleanly

  • Sales Area GE01 / 10 / 00 is linked properly

  • Plant GE01 is integrated and linked with shipping points, storage locations, sales organizations, and purchasing organization

  • the corresponding CBC assignment is present

In Stark Tower, DE01 is visible in Organizational Structure, but the expected CBC-generated assignment is missing. That is why I suspect the issue is not just transactional, but structural.

My questions

  1. Which minimum org structure is required so that a sales organization appears in "Assign Sales Organization - Distribution Channel - Plant"?

  2. Which relationships must be correct between:

    • Company Code

    • Sales Organization

    • Sales Area

    • Distribution Channel

    • Division

    • Plant

    • Shipping Point

    • Storage Location

  3. If all those objects are visible in Organizational Structure, but the sales organization is still missing in that CBC activity, what does that usually mean?

  4. Does that clearly indicate an incomplete or inconsistent org hierarchy?

  5. Is there a reference example of a minimal correct hierarchy for such a setup in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

I attached screenshots from both systems and labeled them clearly as:

  • Arc Reactor (development tenant)

  • Stark Tower (customizing tenant)

My goal is not just to find a workaround, but to understand the correct structural prerequisite so I can rebuild or correct the customizing setup properly.

Thank you.

Screenshots:

Organizational Structure Customizing System (Stark Tower):

___________________________________________________________

Organizational Structure Development System (Arc Reactor):

___________________________________________________________

Assign Sales Organization - Distribution Channel – Plant (Configuration)

entry.title: Completed course not added to learning history- Scorm 1.2 content
entry.content:
Hi,

We have an issue in LMS when a user launches a course (part of a program): the completion confirmation says the course has been passed and is now part of your learning history. However, the course is not added to the learning history/remains in the users learning plan, and the program remains incomplete as a result. This happens to only a single user, and others are able to launch the course and complete it. We tried removing and reassigning the program to the user and assigned the course separately as well, but still the issue persists every time the course is launched. 

I have followed this note 2316953 - Troubleshooting Course/Content Not Marked Completed in SAP SuccessFactors Learning and checked the course settings, it looks good. Retrieved the AICC Data value for the item completion using story report and it says [PLATEAU CONTROL]
FIRSTTIME=0
WAS_SUSPENDED=1
[CORE]
LESSON_LOCATION=index.html#/lessons/xxxxxxxxxxxx
SCORE=,
LESSON_STATUS=INCOMPLETE,SUSPEND.

TIME=

Also after debugging, got the below error in the har file." "error.OnlineContent.ScormCommitFailed\": \"A request to record your progress in the Learning application has failed. Please check your internet connection". 
Appreciate any inputs on how this issue can be resolved.

Thank you & Best Regards,

Meera

entry.title: Re: U.S SECURE 2.0 Act - Section 603
entry.content:
Hello @amrice,

Thank you for the detailed explanation. From what you're describing, it seems the issue is related to the rollover not occurring at the expected time. This can happen due to various system configurations. Some potential causes are described in the KBA 3677876 (Rollover is not happening for Section 603)

If you've already reviewed this document and the issue continues, please open a case for PA-PA-US-BN as it will be necessary to replicate the scenario in your environment for a more detailed investigation. 

Best regards,
Gisele

entry.title: Choose transport popup - functionality on a custom Fiori Application?
entry.content:
Hi all - We have a custom Fiori application that is based on a older legacy program of ours. With clean clore being the goal alot of our source code has been adjusted to use SAP ABAP cloud released objects. The Fiori application has checkboxes for user selection on the UI and when trigerred -> ABAP back-end will add entries to some custom DB tables that we have. Our old legacy program allowed the user to choose a customizing request via a popup to capture these entries in the transport. The idea is to build out the same functionality on our Fiori Application. Prompt user for customizing transport on the UI and then capture the DB entries to the selected transport. 

We used the below function modules for the transport functionality in our old legacy program that I believe are no longer clean core compatabile/not released for public cloud. Has anybody had a similar requirement in the past? Does SAP have released cloud objects to replace the older function modules? Doing some research into this at the moment. 
CALL FUNCTION 'TRINT_ORDER_CHOICE'
CALL FUNCTION 'TR_OBJECTS_INSERT'

entry.title: Re: Deep Dive into SAP Datasphere Object Store of BDC: Benefits, Architecture and implementation
entry.content:
Thanks for the article.

So the Point:

 

  •  Support of local type “initial load” only in Replication Flows is not supported.

This means when you are following the vision of architecture to have all data loaded to an object store it must have CDC capability. So all the CDS View which are enabled for Data Extraction but not enabled for Delta Extraction can not loaded into Object store and you have to go - on top - over a Expensive Hana Space?

This does not sound good for an architecture Design over all.

entry.title: Copying docket number from delivery to FO
entry.content:
Hello,

we have the following scenario. We are on basic TM which is embedded on premise and so we have delivery based FU. A truck is loaded with goods for two ship to parties. There two Dockets  one for each ship to. Docket charges are fixed which is USD 100 for 1 docket. In our case , for 2 dockets it comes to USD 200 (2  deliveries mean two different dockets )

The docket number is added in Delivery document. There is one docket for every delivery. More than one delivery can be in same docket for  deliveries for same ship to party.

we want to copy docket number from both deliveries to FO and want charge management to calculate docket charge type based on Number of dockets ?

Any idea how this can be done ?

rgds

sachin

SAP Inside Track Philadelphia 2026 , Summer 2026!

SAP Inside Track Philadelphia will once again take place at SAP America Headquarters in Newtown Square.

Come network with your peers, listen and share with experts in a relaxed collaborative environment.

2018
2012
2010

The event has been held in several cities around the world for over 15 years.

Learn more about how to hold your SAP Inside Track event in your city through this LINK.

Location:  SAP America, Newtown Square, PA

There are currently no plans to stream the event, however if there is enough demand it will be considered.

Modernizing to power AI with SAP Business Data Cloud

AI’s demand for data has shifted data management strategies. How can you protect your critical business data while modernizing it to power AI?  

In this webinar, hear from global wine and spirits leader Brown-Forman, as they share their experience of moving from SAP Business Warehouse (BW) to SAP Business Data Cloud. Discover a clear path that safeguards existing SAP BW investments while making context-rich data more accessible to AI. You’ll see a live product demonstration and learn about:  

•    Democratizing data access and harmonizing analytics across the organization

•    Creating AI-ready data products with the data product generator for SAP Business Data Cloud  

•    Accelerating innovation and AI workloads using SAP BW data in SAP Business Data Cloud  

Get ready to put your business data to work in the age of AI.

entry.title: Syncing Invoices (Accounts Receivable)/Bills (Accounts Payable) via OData V4?
entry.content:
Hello,

I’m looking to sync Accounts Receivable (Invoices) and Accounts Payable (Bills) from SAP S/4HANA via APIs, and also need to capture deletions for these objects.

From the documentation, it appears that:

  • Invoices may only be available via OData V4 APIs
  • Bills may only be accessible via OData V2 APIs

Can anyone confirm if this is correct?

Additionally, I’m interested in tracking deletions. Some OData V4 APIs return delta tokens, but it’s unclear whether delta handling (including deletions) is supported for Invoices and Bills specifically.

Any clarification before I begin implementation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Restricting over goods receipt quantity during CO11N confirmation
entry.content:
Hi,

During production order confirmation in CO11N / Confirm Production Order Operation, the business uses automatic goods receipt.

The expectation is that the goods receipt quantity should not exceed the production order quantity.

Thanks and regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Restricting over goods receipt quantity during CO11N...
entry.content:
Hi,

In the standard automatic goods receipt scenario, the system expects the goods receipt quantity to correspond to the confirmed yield quantity. However, this is not the same as having a separate standard control to make the quantity in the Goods Movements section non-editable. For additional information about the standard scenario on posting goods receipt for a production order, please refer to Posting Goods Receipts for Production Orders).

So the important distinction is:

  • The standard confirmation tolerance check controls the yield quantity;
  • The customer requirement is to control the editable GR quantity line in Goods Movements during confirmation.

Therefore, even if overdelivery tolerance is maintained, this does not necessarily mean that an error will be raised when only the GR quantity is manually changed in the Goods Movements tab.

Based on this understanding, we are not aware of a standard configuration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that directly prevents users from changing the automatic GR quantity in the Goods Movements section during CO11N / confirmation, or that independently validates that field against the order quantity.

Possible alternatives are:

  1. Use separate GR posting
    Instead of automatic GR during confirmation, the goods receipt can be posted separately via Post Goods Movement / MIGO. In that process, the posted GR quantity is checked directly against the production order tolerance logic (Please refer to Posting Goods Receipts for Production Orders).
  2. Evaluate extensibility
    If stricter control is required specifically for the quantity entered in the Goods Movements section during confirmation, this may need to be evaluated through Custom Logic / released BAdI, since this would go beyond the standard tolerance-based confirmation behavior. One possible option to evaluate is a Custom Logic implementation such as BD_MFGORDER_CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE. Please also refer to the SAP Help Portal documentation Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for Key User Extensibility and Custom Logic – Help Portal.

Thanks and regards.

entry.title: Restrictions by company code and Plant in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
The client needs to restrict access at the company code and Plant level based on the end user's line of business to ensure they only have visibility in their current location within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

The client has 11 Plants and 2 company codes.

Let's assume the end user belongs to the Purchasing business line and is assigned to the Mexico City plant, which represents 1010 in SAP, while the company code is 1000.
The following standard roles are those used by the end user to run Fiori applications in the purchasing area.

Roles estándar

BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST

BR_CASH_MANAGER

BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT

BR_EMPLOYEE

BR_INVENTORY_MANAGER

BR_MATL_PLNR_EXT_PROC

BR_RECEIVING_SPECIALIST

BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_MX

BR_BPC_EXPERT

BR_PURCHASER_RFM

After that, I entered an app called “Maintain Business Roles” (F1492)

Roles in blue generated a copy of the standard because they can be restricted at the plant level (WERKS) and the company code level (BUKRS).

Roles Copia 1

Restriccion

BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST

 

BR_CASH_MANAGER_CDMX_1010

2000

BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_EMPLOYEE

 

BR_INVENTORY_MANAGER_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_MATL_PLNR_EXT_PROC_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_RECEIVING_SPECIALIST_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

1010, 1000

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_MX_CDMX_1010

2000

BR_BPC_EXPERT_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_PURCHASER_RFM_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

Taking as an example one of the 10 roles, BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT, which generates 2 copies, the standard role has no restrictions, but the 2 copies as BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010 and BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010_V2 do have restrictions.

I clicked on edit and for the BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010 role I started with the restrictions.

Then click on “Maintain Restrictions”

In the “Access Categories” section, select “Restricted” for Write, Read, Value Help, and in the Assigned Restriction Types section, click on “general” where you will find the Company code and Plant fields.

For the company field, it is necessary that the end user only see the information for company code 1000, so only place the check mark for 2000 as a restriction.

In the plant field, select all plants except 1010, which represents Mexico City, because I want the user to only have access to that plant and be restricted from the others: 1020, 1030, 1040, 1050, 1060, etc.

Finally, I clicked the "Save" button to save the changes.

I repeated the steps with the rest of the roles, creating a copy respectively, in which I only restricted the company code and the plant

entry.title: In-App Update Pop-Up Appears Daily in SAP Cloud for Customer Mobile App
entry.content:
When using MDM to manage updates of the SAP Cloud for Customer mobile app, many customers expect that update pop-ups will no longer appear for end users. However, it is quite common to still see the update message showing every day when opening the app, even in fully managed environments.

At first, this can be confusing and may look like a system issue. In reality, this behavior is caused by a standard configuration in the system. The app version check is turned on by default and works independently from MDM. This means the system does not recognize that the app version is already being controlled centrally.

Because of this, users may continue to receive update prompts, even when they are already using the correct and latest version distributed by the company. This can create unnecessary interruptions and raise concerns from business users.

It is important to understand that this feature is designed to help in environments where there is no central control. In those cases, it ensures that users stay on a compatible version of the application. However, when MDM is in place, this extra check becomes redundant since version control is already handled externally.

To improve the user experience, this setting should be adjusted. By turning off the app version check and allowing updates to be managed by the administrator, the daily pop-ups can be removed and the system behavior will better match the company’s setup.

Once adjusted, users can work without unnecessary prompts while still keeping full control over app versions.

entry.title: Re: Best Practice for Intercompany Project Timesheets in...
entry.content:
Direct intercompany charging has been possible in FI and PS for a very long time. The new part is the real-time margin posting in addition to the internal cost posting.

The intercompany direct charging is configured in FI.  You have to allow it between the 2 company codes.  Both Company Codes have to be in the same Controlling Area.  Look for "Define Intercompany Clearing Accounts" and check with your FI expert.

Review this blog for the latest: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/intercompany-cost-allocations-in-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13442721

It’s available in private cloud and on premise, you have to activate a business function (FINS_CO_ICO_PROC_ENH_101).  Looks like it first became available in S/4HANA 1610

entry.title: Re: Completed course not added to learning history- Scorm 1.2 content
entry.content:
Hi @sysfpathforward ,

It might be interesting to fetch Content Debug Logs while the user is trying to complete the content (https://me.sap.com/notes/2978324), in order to see what exactly the content is communicating to LMS when the user finishes / leaves the content. Please note that for LESSON_STATUS such as INCOMPLETE or FAILED, is expected that LMS won't grant a completion to the user, since the status communicated is a failure.

If a failure is being sent by the content, then maybe the user is clicking on the wrong button to leave the content, for example (some contents are designed to close properly only when clicking on specific "X" or "Exit" buttons, and this is configured by the content vendor and is not controlled by LMS), or maybe the users hasn't achieved the required SCORE (in case one is needed). These are examples, these are not the only causes for such scenario.

Best regards.

entry.title: Is MCP Necessary? Lessons from AI-Assisted SAP Datasphere Modeling
entry.content:
Abstract: This article explores whether MCP is necessary when automating SAP Datasphere with AI. With SAP's official CLI already available, is building an MCP Server the right approach — or can AI simply call the CLI directly?

Disclaimer: This article represents the author's personal views and practical experience only, and does not represent the position of any company or organization. The technical analysis and cost calculations are based on specific scenarios and are for reference only.

1. Background

1.1 The Scenario

The goal was to use AI to automate SAP Datasphere operations, such as creating tables, views, and analytic models. For more details on this approach, see my previous article: Using Claude Code and Natural Language to Create SAP Datasphere Artifacts.

SAP provides an official command-line tool @sap/datasphere-cli, a fully-featured Node.js CLI that covers the main Datasphere operations:

Main Modules:

  • objects - Manage Datasphere objects (tables, views, analytic models, data flows, etc.) including create, read, update, delete, and deploy
  • spaces - Space management, including creation, configuration, and member management
  • dbusers - Database user management, including user creation, password reset, and permission configuration
  • marketplace - Data marketplace product installation and management
  • tasks - Task chain execution and monitoring
  • cache - Login credential cache management

Command Examples:

# Read the complete JSON structure of an existing object
datasphere objects views read --name EXISTING_VIEW --space MYSPACE

# Create a new object from a JSON file
datasphere objects tables create --file-path table.json --space MYSPACE

# Deploy objects to a specified space
datasphere objects deploy --space MYSPACE

# List database users in a space
datasphere dbusers list --space MYSPACE

# Run a task chain
datasphere tasks chains run --space MYSPACE --name DAILY_ETL

The CLI supports the --output json parameter, outputting structured JSON format, which is ideal for programmatic processing and AI parsing.

1.2 The Question

How do we enable AI (Claude) to call this CLI?

Two mainstream approaches:

  • Approach A: Develop an MCP Server to wrap CLI functions as MCP tools for AI clients
  • Approach B: Use Claude Code Agent to call CLI directly

2. MCP Approach Analysis

2.1 What is MCP

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standardized protocol proposed by Anthropic that enables AI to interact with external tools:

User → Claude Desktop → MCP Server (separate process) → Backend Services
              ↑
         JSON-RPC 2.0
         over stdio

2.2 Existing MCP Implementation

Before deciding to develop my own, I researched existing solutions. There's already an open-source project mariodefe/sap-datasphere-mcp on GitHub that implements an MCP Server for SAP Datasphere. Following the principle of not reinventing the wheel, I analyzed this project to see if it could be used directly.

The project provides 48 MCP tools covering:

Module Tools Operations

Space Management4List spaces, get space info, get space assets, etc.
Data Query6Execute SQL queries, smart queries, relational/analytical queries, etc.
Metadata Browsing12Search tables, get table schema, column distribution analysis, asset catalog browsing, etc.
Database User Management5Create/update/delete users, reset passwords
Task Management4Run task chains, view task logs, task history, etc.
Connection Management3List data source connections, test connections
Data Marketplace2Browse marketplace packages
Other12Tenant info, user info, OAuth scopes, search, etc.

2.3 MCP's Token Consumption Problem

The MCP protocol requires returning all tool descriptions via tools/list at initialization. I analyzed the actual data from this project:

  • Total tool description characters: ~62,000 characters
  • Estimated token count: ~15,500 tokens

Problem: These 15,500 tokens are counted in the context at the start of every conversation. Even if the user only wants to perform 1 simple operation, they pay for all 48 tool descriptions — this is a "hidden tax."

Using Claude Sonnet 4.6 as an example ($3/million input tokens), MCP initialization consumes ~15,500 tokens:

Frequency MCP Initialization Cost

Per session$0.047
20 times/day$0.93
Per month$27.90
Per year$339

Note: The above only calculates MCP tool description initialization costs, excluding actual conversation content and output. Pricing data from Anthropic official documentation (April 2026).

3. Alternative: Using Claude Code Agent to Call CLI Directly

3.1 Architecture Comparison

MCP Architecture:

Claude Desktop / Cursor / Other AI Clients
    ↓
JSON-RPC over stdio
    ↓
MCP Server (separate process, 8000+ lines of code)
    ↓
OAuth Handler / CLI Wrapper
    ↓
SAP Datasphere API / CLI

Agent Architecture:

Claude Code
    ↓
Bash tool (direct call)
    ↓
SAP Datasphere CLI

3.2 Advantages of the Agent Approach

Comparison MCP (48 tools) Agent

Initialization Tokens~15,5000
Development ComplexityHigh (requires understanding MCP protocol, JSON-RPC, OAuth, etc.)Low (define Skill + call CLI)
Separate ProcessRequiredNot required
Maintenance CostHigh (CLI updates require code changes and release)Low (usually auto-compatible)
Debugging DifficultyHigh (stdio communication)Low (direct Bash output)

3.3 Why CLI is Naturally Suited for AI Agents

CLI commands are inherently atomic operations:

datasphere objects tables create   # Create a table
datasphere objects views read      # Read a view
datasphere objects deploy          # Deploy an object
datasphere dbusers list            # List database users

This fragmented design aligns perfectly with how AI Agents work:

  1. Understand Intent - User says "create a sales analysis model"
  2. Decompose Task - AI breaks it down: create fact table → create dimension tables → create view → create model
  3. Execute Step by Step - Call CLI commands one by one
  4. Adapt Dynamically - Step failed? Read the error message, auto-correct and retry

MCP tries to "package" these atomic operations into predefined composite tools, which actually:

  • Limits AI flexibility
  • Cannot handle unforeseen scenarios
  • Makes it harder to identify which step failed

CLI's fragmentation + AI's reasoning ability = flexible and controllable automation.

4. Other Issues with MCP

4.1 Maintenance Cost After CLI Updates

SAP Datasphere CLI receives regular updates. With MCP:

CLI Update
  → MCP Server code changes
  → Tool description updates
  → inputSchema adjustments
  → Test case updates
  → npm/pypi release new version
  → Users update dependencies

Taking sap-datasphere-mcp as an example, the project currently doesn't implement CLI version detection — it neither checks the currently installed CLI version nor prompts users if the CLI needs updating. This means when SAP releases a new CLI version, users may encounter compatibility issues without knowing why.

With Agent directly calling CLI:

  • Claude can directly read datasphere --help to get the latest commands
  • No additional adaptation layer needed
  • Automatically adapts to new features

4.2 MCP Server is Essentially a "Translation Layer"

Claude Request → JSON-RPC → MCP Server → CLI Command → Parse Output → JSON-RPC → Claude

This translation layer requires:

  1. Parsing Claude's JSON request
  2. Mapping to CLI commands
  3. Executing CLI
  4. Parsing CLI output
  5. Reformatting as MCP response

Problems:

  • This translation layer adds complexity and potential errors
  • CLI output format changes can cause parsing failures
  • Need to handle all CLI error cases

4.3 Limited Flexibility

MCP requires predefined all possible operations. This means:

  • Developers must anticipate all possible user needs in advance
  • Each operation requires defining tool name, description, and parameter structure
  • When new requirements emerge, code must be modified and a new version released

Taking sap-datasphere-mcp's 48 tools as an example, if a user wants to execute a CLI command that hasn't been wrapped (like a feature added in a new CLI version), they cannot do it through MCP and must wait for the project to update.

Claude Code Agent doesn't have this limitation — it can directly read datasphere --help, understand user intent, dynamically construct commands, and auto-adjust and retry on errors. Whatever the CLI can do, the AI can use.

5. When Does MCP Make Sense?

5.1 Scenarios Where MCP Fits

Scenario Reason

Services without CLICan only access via REST API
Cross-client ReuseMultiple clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.) using the same MCP Server
Enterprise StandardizationOrganization needs unified toolsets and permission control
Strict Audit RequirementsNeed to log and audit all AI operations

5.2 Scenarios Where MCP is Not Needed

Scenario Reason

Well-established CLI ExistsDirect calling is simpler and more efficient
Fast IterationSkill modifications take effect immediately, no release process needed
Cost SensitiveMCP initialization tokens are an ongoing hidden cost
Individual/Small TeamNo need for cross-client reuse

6. Conclusion

6.1 For the SAP Datasphere Scenario

Based on my practice, with a well-established CLI available, I chose Claude Code Agent over MCP. Key considerations:

  • SAP provides a fully-featured CLI (@sap/datasphere-cli)
  • CLI supports JSON output, AI can parse directly
  • Agent approach is more flexible, can automatically adapt to CLI updates
  • Avoids MCP initialization token consumption

But this is just my choice based on specific requirements, and may not apply to all scenarios.

6.2 Open Question

While writing this article, I've been thinking about a question: As AI capabilities continue to grow, what is the long-term value of "middleware" like MCP?

When AI can already understand CLI documentation, parse JSON output, and auto-adjust based on errors, do we still need a predefined tool layer? Will MCP's value increase as AI capabilities improve, or will it gradually be replaced by more flexible Agent approaches?

Perhaps time will tell. Feel free to share your perspective!

entry.title: Custom Joule Agent for SAP Ariba SLP - Building an AI Agent for Supplier Registration Approval
entry.content:
The Premise

I'll be honest — I've built this exact workflow before.

In previous posts, I put together a supplier registration approval automation using SAP Build Process Automation. It worked, but it took me a significant amount of time to complete: process flows, variables, connections, data mappings, error handling — each piece designed and wired manually. You can check out those articles Extending SLP functionalities with SAP BTP and Artificial Intelligence  and SAP Ariba SLP - Optical character recognition and Extraction for Supplier Registration Process .

This time, I completed a fully functional proof of concept in less than 7 days.

Same business problem. Same outcome. A fraction of the effort.

Going in, I had all the questions a developer instinctively asks: Who manages the data and how? Where do variables live? How are connections handled? What happens when something fails? These are the questions that typically consume the first week of any integration project before a single line of logic is written.

With Joule Studio, most of those questions answered themselves. The agent managed context between steps, retained the values I needed across the workflow, and handled the SAP Ariba skill connections natively — often without me explicitly instructing it to. The infrastructure just disappeared.

What remained was the part that actually matters: defining the business logic, the scoring model, the decision rules, and the communication to the supplier. The work that requires domain knowledge, not plumbing.

The Real Cost of Manual Supplier Review

Every supplier onboarding in SAP Ariba SLP follows the same predictable path. The supplier fills out a registration questionnaire. An approval task lands in someone's queue. That someone opens it, reads through the answers, checks the numbers, looks for the documents, and makes a call: approve, send it back, or reject.

Sounds simple. It really doesn't feel that way at month-end when the queue has 40 tasks in it.

Here's what always got me: most of what a reviewer is doing in that queue isn't actually a judgment call. They're reading a free-text answer asking themselves — does this BCP response mention an RPO? Does this AML section describe a whistleblowing channel? They're checking if a growth rate in one field matches a balance change in another. They're confirming a document is attached. That's not analysis. That's a checklist. A very slow, very manual, very inconsistent checklist being run by people whose time is worth a lot more than that.

And the problems that came from it were real:

  • Reviews varied wildly depending on who did them — the same questionnaire could sail through with one reviewer and generate three rounds of back-and-forth with another, often over a misspelled word or an answer that was technically correct but phrased oddly.
  • Tasks just sat there — not because nobody cared, but because reviewers had other priorities and supplier registration wasn't always the urgent thing. Suppliers would wait days on submissions that should have been a five-minute review.
  • Nothing was documented — approvals went through with no written rationale. The moment someone asked "why was this supplier approved?" the answer was essentially "because someone clicked approve."
  • Language became a routing problem — suppliers in Argentina or Brazil submitting in Spanish or Portuguese meant we couldn't just assign the task to anyone. It either waited for the right person or got reviewed by someone who couldn't properly read the answers.

None of these are hard problems. They're consistency problems. And the fix isn't hiring more reviewers — it's stopping the pattern-matching work from reaching a human in the first place.

That's where this started.

How it works?

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

How it looks?

 

How can I build my own?

Before anything else, you'll need to set up Joule Studio inside SAP Build. If you haven't done that yet, this guide walks you through it - How to get Started with Joule Studio 

Once you're in, you'll notice two new options in the Build lobby: Create a Joule Agent and Create a Joule Skill. Think of skills as the hands of the agent — they are the individual actions it can take. The agent is the brain that decides when and how to use them.

For this project, we built 4 custom skills, each one mapped to a specific SAP Ariba API call:

SkillWhat It Does
getPendingTasksRetrieves all active approval tasks from SAP Ariba SLP
getTaskDetailsFetches full task data including document ID, URL, and approval chain
getQuestionsAnswersPulls the supplier's questionnaire responses by document ID
postDecisionPosts the approval decision and comment back to the task in Ariba

These four skills are the only connection points between the agent and SAP Ariba. Everything else — the analysis, the scoring, the decision logic, the multilingual handling — lives entirely inside the agent configuration. No additional middleware, no custom code.

In Joule Studio, the foundation of every skill is the destination and the action endpoint. This is fundamentally different from how SAP Build Process Automation handles connectivity. In BPA, you could get away with loosely configured connections and patch things as you went. In Joule Studio, that doesn't work. Each skill needs a correctly defined destination in SAP BTP — the right authentication, the right base URL, the right headers — and each action within that skill needs a precisely mapped endpoint. Get either of those wrong and the skill fails silently, or worse, returns data you can't trust.

Check the SAP BTP Destinations section, it will give you the base for setting Destinations - End to End guide to setup Joule, SAP Build Process Automation, and SAP Build Work Zone on SAP BTP 

Let's make this concrete. Below is the actual configuration of getPendingTasks — the first skill the agent calls every time it runs. On the left you can see the destination binding pointing to the SAP Ariba environment. Below it, the action defines the specific endpoint the skill hits, the HTTP method, and the parameters it passes. This is the complete bridge between Joule Studio and Ariba. No code, no transformation layer — just a correctly configured destination and a precisely defined action

getPendingTasks Joule Skill

Pending Task Destination Response

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

What about the AI Agent?

Once your skills are ready, you create the agent. Joule Studio gives you three sections to configure. Each one has a distinct job — and understanding the boundary between them is what makes the difference between an agent that works reliably and one that kind of works most of the time.

 

Expertise — Who the agent is

This is where you define the agent's identity, role, and domain knowledge. Not what it does step by step, but what it knows going in. Think of it as the briefing you'd give a new analyst before their first day on the job.

In our case, we defined the agent as Joule, embedded in SAP Ariba SLP, with deep knowledge of supplier onboarding workflows, approval mechanics, questionnaire interpretation, and the SLP object model. We spelled out the domain concepts — tasks, workspaces, approval requests, registration projects — so the agent could interpret ambiguous API data through the right lens. When a financial number looked inconsistent, it needed procurement context to judge it correctly. This section provided that foundation.

Instructions — What the agent does

This is the behavioral core. A sequential set of rules that tells the agent exactly how to act, in what order, and under what conditions. Every step, every trigger, every output format lives here.

In our case, this section contained the full seven-step workflow: filtering tasks, building the results table, triggering the questionnaire analysis on a specific user command, running the three-part scoring analysis automatically, and posting the decision without waiting for confirmation. We also defined the comment templates for each decision type and the language rule for multilingual suppliers here. If the Expertise section is the briefing, Instructions is the playbook.

Additional Context — The rules the agent looks up

This is the reference layer. Static mappings, lookup tables, scoring rubrics, decision logic, and field definitions that the agent consults during execution. It doesn't drive behavior directly — Instructions does that — but it gives the agent the structured data it needs to make the right calls.

In our case, this section held everything that would be too verbose to embed in Instructions: the full decision mapping (Approve / Deny / DenyWithResubmit with priority rules), the answer lookup table mapping qualifiedNumber fields to their meaning, the complete scoring checklists for all four questions, the financial flag thresholds, the compliance flag definitions, and the language rule for comment output. Keeping this separate from Instructions made both sections easier to read, easier to update, and easier to debug when something didn't behave as expected.

TIP💡 A rule of thumb that saved us a lot of iteration: if it describes behavior, it belongs in Instructions. If it describes data or rules the behavior depends on, it belongs in Additional Context. Mixing the two is the most common reason agent configurations become hard to maintain.

Final Thoughts

I want to be honest about something before wrapping this up.

This agent doesn't replace human judgment. It was never meant to. There will always be submissions that land in a grey area — answers that technically check the boxes but still feel incomplete, numbers that pass the threshold but don't quite add up, situations that no rulebook covers cleanly. Those still need a person to look at them, for that we can add a "Human node" on Ariba SLP to make sure a human reviews AI decision.

What the agent does is make sure those are the only cases that reach a human. Everything else — the clean submissions, the straightforward approvals, the obvious denials — gets handled automatically, documented properly, and closed. In most onboarding queues, that's the majority of the work.

What genuinely surprised me wasn't the time I saved, though going from a much longer build down to seven days is hard to dismiss. It was how differently I had to think about the problem.

A few things I'd tell you to pay attention to before you start:

  • Do not rush the destinations. I know it looks like a simple setup step. It isn't. Get the BTP destination and the endpoint actions right before you touch anything else. Every problem I had early on traced back to this. HERE IS WHERE YOU DATA TYPE WILL BE DEFINED.
  • Put the real work into Additional Context. The scoring rubrics, the decision mapping, the language rules — that's where the agent gets smart. A generic configuration gives you a generic agent. The domain-specific rules are what make it actually useful.
  • Test with messy data, not just happy paths. Run a clean approval, a DenyWithResubmit with missing elements, a hard Deny, and at least one Spanish or Portuguese submission. If those four scenarios work correctly, you're in good shape.
  • Always filter on title, not description. The description field can come back empty from the API. I learned this the hard way so you don't have to.

Last but no least let me share some value insights of this PoC

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (003) - OSのアップデートとアップグレード
entry.content:
システムのOSは、End of Life(以下、EOL)を迎える前にバージョンを更新することが、システム運用において重要なタスクの一つです。

OSバージョンがEOLを過ぎると、新しいセキュリティパッチが提供されないこと、またテクニカルサポートが十分に提供されないことから、SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private EditionではTop Issueとしてもお客様にOSバージョンの更新を依頼しております。

本記事では、SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionをご利用の際のOSバージョン管理についてご紹介します。


SAP for Meにて現在利用中のOSバージョンを確認する方法

SAP for Meへログイン後、以下の手順で確認できます。
「Services & Support」→「Private Cloud Workspace」→「Landscape Management」セクションの「My Systems」をクリックします。

また、以下のURLへアクセスすることで、お客様のシステムリストを確認することも可能です。
https://me.sap.com/privcloudlandscape2#?variant=standard

特定SIDのOSバージョンは、対象システムのSIDをクリックし、Factsheetタブにて確認できます。

下記はサンプル画面となります。

 

全システムの情報は、［Download Tables］をクリックすることで確認できます。

ダウンロードしたExcelファイルの「OS Version」または「OS Release」列をご確認ください。

 

サポートされるOSバージョン

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private EditionでサポートされるOSバージョンは、SUSEのリリースとは異なります。
理由は、SUSEで新しいバージョンがリリースされた後に、RISE側で該当バージョンへの更新手順の確立および動作確認が必要となるためです。

そのため、該当SRテンプレートで選択可能なサービスパックおよびOSバージョンが基準となることをご承知おきください。

すべての作業に共通しますが、開発・品質保証・本番の順序で作業を計画し、ビジネスシナリオをお客様側でテストすることで、本番環境の安定稼働を確保することを推奨します。

リリース済みのSUSEバージョンおよびEOLは、下記SUSE公式サイトでご確認いただけます。
RISEでのEOLは、SUSE公式サイトに掲載されている「LTSS Ends」と同様です。

Product Support Lifecycle | SUSE

 

下記は、SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15のサポート期限の例です。

 

利用するSRテンプレート

SRにてOSのアップデート／アップグレードをご紹介する前に、用語の定義を行います。

  • アップデート: メジャーバージョンは変更せず、マイナーバージョン（サービスパック）を更新すること。
    例：SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 → SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP5
  • アップグレード: メジャーバージョンを変更すること。
    例：SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 12 → SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15

なお、本記事に記載のSR作成画面は、記事作成時点のものです

 

OSのアップデート

SRテンプレート: Update OS Service Pack (Linux)

  • Current OS version: ご利用中のバージョンが自動的に表示されます。
  • Target Service Pack version: アップデートのターゲットバージョンを選択してください。
  • Set system message (for ABAP systems):  ABAPシステムにおいて、メンテナンス期間中に表示するシステムメッセージを設定するかどうかを指定する項目です。

 

 

OSのアップグレード

SRテンプレート: Upgrade SLES OS to Major Version

 

  • Current OS version: ご利用中のバージョンが自動的に表示されます。
  • Target OS version: アップデートのターゲットバージョンを選択してください。
  • Set system message (for ABAP systems):  ABAPシステムにおいて、メンテナンス期間中に表示するシステムメッセージを設定するかどうかを指定する項目です。

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private EditionでサポートされるSUSEの最新メジャーバージョンは現時点で15のため、Target OS versionでは選択可能な項目がありません。

 

まとめ

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionでは、OSバージョンをEOL前に更新することが重要です。

OSの更新には、サービスパック更新の「アップデート」とメジャーバージョン更新の「アップグレード」があります。

現在のOSバージョンはSAP for Meで確認でき、サポート対象OSバージョンはSAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionで定められております。

更新作業は開発・品質保証・本番環境の順序で計画し、テストを実施することで本番環境の安定稼働を確保することが推奨されます。

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: Extract, Transform & Load (ETL) - Best Practices
entry.content:
@Nich_QS01 - Thank you for the questions:

1. This is known that SAP replication flow does not support SAP Datasphere to SAP Datasphere replication. While recommendation is to use transformation for such use cases. There is also a concept of view persistency. However Replication flow/transformation does not allow currently to replicate the data from SAP Datasphere graphical or SQL views into local table in SAP Datasphere (Workaround: SAP Datasphere, HANA cloud connection can be created and used a source into Replication flow with the know limitations). Until native support is available, using a Data flow is a practical option. Alternatively, you may consider submitting a request through the Customer Influence portal.

2. Remote Table replication is based on SAP HANA SDI technology and relies on Data Provisioning Agent (DP Agent) with specific adapters (such as HANA or ABAP adapters). It is essentially a carry-forward capability from SAP HANA into SAP Datasphere, with some known limitations. This approach works best when the target is primarily SAP HANA, but it introduces additional dependency on agents and adapters.In contrast, Replication Flows are native to SAP Datasphere and do not depend on DP Agent or adapters for cloud to cloud scenarios. Additionally, Replication Flows (and Data Flows) offer broader ETL capabilities, monitoring, and lifecycle management, making them the recommended option for most scenarios.

entry.title: Re: Joule Skill for S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
To my best understanding, Joule Skills don’t call S/4HANA directly.

It's go through a SAP BTP Destination, which then uses the Communication Arrangement in S/4HANA (SAP_COM_0008, SAP_COM_0053, etc.).  Basically making a service‑to‑service call using the Technical User defined in that destination.

Because of that, authorization is based on the Technical User, not the end user. this is gap.
Whatever a technical user is allowed to see (purchase org, company code, etc.) is what the Skill can access. If you want to restrict data, you do it by assigning the right business roles and restriction values to the Technical User in S/4HANA.

At the moment, Joule Skills do not support Principal Propagation into S/4HANA Public Cloud. So you can’t pass the end user’s identity through a Communication Arrangement. The only supported setup today is via a Technical User or certificate.

This is base my best understanding feel free if this help resolve your problem or anyone else have one better solution. I appreciate.

entry.title: Re: Artificial intelligent
entry.content:
The role of AI in SAP Business One is mainly about helping small and mid‑size businesses automate routine work and make decisions faster.  I thing AI will be better used in areas like:

  1. Predictive insights (sales forecasts, inventory predictions, cash‑flow trends)
  2. Automated document processing (OCR for invoices, purchase orders, receipts)
  3. Chat‑based assistance for searching data or running reports.
  4. Exception alerts (flagging unusual transactions or risks)
  5. Recommendation engines (suggesting items, suppliers, or pricing actions).

SAP smartly and silently adding AI capabilities through SAP Business One Web Client, SAP BTP, and SAP AI services, so customers can extend B1 with machine learning, automation, and natural‑language interactions.

please refer SAP’s official page on AI for SMBs, you might get some more idea (including SAP B1) is here: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_ONE

please feel free, happy to share the best of my knowledge.

entry.title: Re: Quick Actions for Joule
entry.content:
To straight, No — Joule does not require Employee Central Quick Action templates to be configured for transactional use cases. 

let says, for now, Joule in SAP SuccessFactors triggers actions through the existing EC framework, and Quick Actions are supported way for Joule to perform changes like Change of Address, Change of customer Name, etc.

Joule does not bypass EC workflows or write directly into portlets. So:  yes, Quick Actions must exist for Joule to perform the transaction.

To answer your 2nd Q : - No, Joule cannot update the portlet directly without the corresponding Quick Action.

Joule simply invokes the same underlying service that Quick Actions use — it doesn’t introduce a new write mechanism.

In short:
If the Quick Action isn’t configured, Joule can’t execute that transaction.     Pls check and let me know if this help you anyway.

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - RFC Destination on OAuth2
entry.content:
In this situation, SAP S4 works as a client which consumes a service exposed by other service provider system.

Prerequisite

Before configruation, you will get a service key from the service provider including a client id/client secret/token url similar like this

{
  "private_key": "-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----\nMIIEvQIBADANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQEFAASCBKcwggSjAgEAAoIBAQDJ... (actual private key) ...\n-----END PRIVATE KEY-----\n", 
  "client_id": "123456789012345678901", 
  "token_uri": "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token" 
}

Configuration

1. Create a Client Profile

TCODE: SE80

for instance, if the client is to consume a Mulesoft API, we can name it ZMULE as below

2. Configure Profile

TCODE: SOAUTH2_CLIENT

click create button

Select the client profile created just now and input a client id from the service key you got from the service provider.

in our example, the client id in the Prequisite section is '123456789012345678901'.

then fill in the token url

in the next tab fill in the client secret

save the configuration.

3. Unit Test

The easist way to conduct a connectivity test is via RFC Destination

TCODE: SM59

Get a working endpoint from the service provider and fill the URL into the RFC destination with type G

In the Logon & Security tab, select the client profile configured in the last step.

in the security section, activate SSL and select the view containing the trusted server certificate of service provider system.

click on button 'Connection Test'

you will get a view like this showing that authentication is working. On the other hand, you need to check in the service provider system if any incoming traffic.

Once the RFC destiantion is ready, you can use it as a building block in 

  • Intergation server configuration in SXMB_ADM, or
  • Service Consumer configuration in SOAMANAGER manually, or
  • HTTP_CLIENT in ABAP, etc

 

However, By hard coding the URL, ABAP can still implement an http client consuming a service, similar like this

DATA: lo_http_client TYPE REF TO if_http_client,
      lv_url         TYPE string VALUE 'https://example.com',
      lv_response    TYPE string.

cl_http_client=>create_by_url(
  EXPORTING
    url                = lv_url
  IMPORTING
    client             = lo_http_client
  EXCEPTIONS
    argument_not_found = 1
    plugin_not_active  = 2
    internal_error     = 3
    OTHERS             = 4 ).

However this is not a recommended way. The connection information is better to be configurable so that when the content has been transported into susequential systems like QA, Pre-Prod or Prod, it would be more flexible to update

entry.title: Re: AI capabilities for custom Application
entry.content:
@Abinaya05  ... @meenakshignanakannan  view is really good and clear and I like it.

My suggest is :  

To answer your Q, yes, the scenario is possible, but with one important clarification:

Joule itself cannot be embedded directly inside a custom Fiori or RAP app as on today today, hopefully one day and again that's best of my understand , if someone have better understanding pls feel free to correct.

What is possible "Is to add GenAI capabilities around your app using SAP AI Core, SAP AI Services, or an LLM hosted on BTP. Your app provides the business data (via OData), and the AI layer provides the natural‑language insights.

For your use case (AI answering questions about the list report):

Expose the report data through your OData service

Build a small BTP service that performs grounding/RAG on that data

Call your AI service from the UI5 app and return the answer to the user

If you are building a RAP‑based app, the integration is even cleaner because RAP gives you structured APIs and better extensibility for AI scenarios.

So in short:
Lift & Shift → Yes, possible
RAP App → Even better for AI integration
Direct Joule embedding → Not supported yet, but custom GenAI integration is fully achievable  

Feel free to respond if this help.. Happy learning.

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - RFC Destination on Client Certificate
entry.content:
In this case, SAP S/4 HANA works as a client. It consumes service from provider system. 

Prerequisite

install openssl

here are the steps for configuration

1. Generate Private key by openssl

openssl genrsa -out private.key 2048

2. Generate a certificate by openssl

openssl req -new -x509 -key private.key -out client.crt -days 365

here is an example. The custom value has been highlighted.

  • Country Name (2 letter code) [AU]:AU
  • State or Province Name (full name) [Some-State]:Victoria
  • Locality Name (eg, city) []:Melbourne
  • Organization Name (eg, company) [Internet Widgits Pty Ltd]:ServiceProvider
  • Organizational Unit Name (eg, section) []:IT
  • Common Name (e.g. server FQDN or YOUR name) []:TEST
  • Email Address []:

3. Merge key and certificate into .pfx by openssl

Since SAP doesn't support import private key directly, we can only merge the key file and certificate file into a .pfx file , then import the .pfx file to SAP S4.

openssl pkcs12 -export -out client.pfx -inkey private.key -in client.crt

 4. Upload .pfx into a new PSE in S4

TCODE: STRUST

you maybe need to create a new PSE at first

create a new SSL client identity

import the .pfx file

input the password for accessing the file

import certificate.

after the .pfx file has been imported, double click on the PSE, double click on the subject on the right, the certificate information will be shown in the view below. click the button 'add to cert. list'. The certificate will be placed in the list.

5. Unit Test

since SAP S4 is working as a client, we will create a RFC Destination type G to check the connection.

TCODE: SM59

Create a RFC destination with type G. Fill in the service URL information.

in the Logon & Security tab, check Active option for SSL and in the dropdown menu of SSL Client PSE ID, choose the one created just now.

save the RFC destination and conduct a connection test.

you maybe get an error response like this. as long as the information is coming from the service provider system, it means the authentication is working.

Once the RFC Destination has been configured, it can be used as a building block in many places like:

  • Service Consumer in  SOAMANAGER
  • Integration Engine for middleware like CPI

Conclusion

RFC Destination does support client certificate

entry.title: How to automatically release Outbound Delivery upon Sales Order release (for specific Site and Custo
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to achieve the following requirement in SAP Business ByDesign and need your guidance on the best approach.

**Requirement:**
- **Trigger:** When Sales Order is released
- **Action:** Automatically release Outbound Delivery (with Goods Issue posted)
- **Scope:** For a specific Site and Customer Account combination only

**Questions:**

1. **Is it possible to achieve the above requirement using only standard configuration?**
- If YES, what configuration settings are required?

2. **I have investigated Logistics Model settings and understood as follows. Is my understanding correct?**

**Pattern 1: Standard Shipping WITH tasks**
- Flow: Sales Order → Warehouse Task Creation → Task Confirmation → Outbound Delivery Creation → (Auto Release possible) → Goods Issue
- **Release Outbound Delivery = Automatically**: ✅ Can be configured in Logistics Model
- Trigger: Outbound Delivery is automatically created and released when warehouse task is confirmed
- Reference: KBA 1872028

**Pattern 2: Standard Shipping WITHOUT tasks (PGI method)**
- Flow: Sales Order → PGI execution → Outbound Delivery creation + Goods Issue simultaneously
- **Release Outbound Delivery = Automatically**: ❌ Cannot be configured in Logistics Model
- Error: "You cannot automatically release an outbound delivery if the logistics model does not contain a task"
- Reference: KBA 3503755

3. **If standard configuration cannot achieve this requirement, what are the recommended approaches?**

**Note:** Please respond following the question numbering above for clarity.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide Needed: Migrating Investments from FI-Asset to SAP TRM (No Fiori)
entry.content:
​Hi Experts,

​I need a complete, end-to-end roadmap for a project where we are moving Fixed Term Deposits and Securities out of the FI-Asset Accounting (FI-AA) module and into SAP TRM (S/4HANA).

​We are looking for a "from-scratch" explanation as we want to follow the standard SAP best practice. Please note: We do not have access to Fiori apps for this project; all steps must be performed via SAP GUI.

​Could you please provide the process for the following:

​Retirement: How do we retire these financial "assets" from the FI-AA sub-ledger properly? We need to clear the G/L and sub-ledger without triggering any Gain/Loss, tax impacts, or depreciation errors. What is the specific T-Code and process?

​The Bridge: What is the standard way to "hand over" the balance from the FI module to the Treasury module so the books stay balanced?

​Upload: Since we are using SAP GUI, which migration tools or T-Codes should be used to upload the active deals into TRM? What are the specific objects required?

​We are approaching this with zero baseline knowledge of this specific migration path and want to ensure a clean audit trail. Any checklists or T-Code sequences would be extremely helpful.

​Thanks in advance for your guidance!

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - SOAMANAGER Manual Configuration on Oauth2
entry.content:
In this case, SAP S4 HANA works as a client (service consumer) . The service provider does support OAuth2 authentication.

Prerequisite

an OAuth2 Client Profile should be configured. Please refer to here for detail steps.

Configuration in SOAMANAGER

TCODE: SOAMANAGER

click on the 'web service configuration'

input an outbound interface, say SalesOrderNotification_Out

Create a logical port for this outbound interface. in the step 2, consumer security, select OAuth2.0 and choose the client profile configured just now

The rest of configuration is the same as the steps for normal web service configuration.

entry.title: SAP TM Attachments for field-level scales rate
entry.content:
Hi Expert

Is it possible to create attachments for field-level scales (rate table), as there is a business need for each price in the rates to have an attachment?

SAP TM 2025

Thanks
Romzi
